chapters.title: Fire (Agni)
chapters.content_preview: Fire (Agni)  Gives pleasure and growth. Ideal for festivals, fine arts, farming, wearing new clothes, and music.  Bhadra(Auspicious/Good)  2nd (Dwitiya), 7th (Saptami), 12th (Dwadashi)

chapters.title: Earth (Prithvi)
chapters.content_preview: Earth (Prithvi)  Gives community and stability. Ideal for education, charitable works, starting business, or making peace.  Jaya(Victory/Conquest)  3rd (Tritiya), 8th (Ashtami), 13th (Trayodashi)

chapters.title: Space (Akash)
chapters.content_preview: Space (Akash)  Gives victory and power. Ideal for litigation, battles, sports, legal arguments, and overcoming enemies.  Rikta(Empty/Void)  4th (Chaturthi), 9th (Navami), 14th (Chaturdashi)

chapters.title: Water (Apas)
chapters.content_preview: Water (Apas)  Gives destruction or release. Inauspicious for major beginnings. Best for surgical operations, demolitions, or ending bad habits.  Poorna(Full/Complete)  5th (Panchami), 10th (Dashami), 15th/30th (Purnima/Amavasya)

chapters.title: Air (Vayu)
chapters.content_preview: Air (Vayu)  Gives fulfillment and abundance. Ideal for marriages, religious ceremonies, travel, or finalizing contracts. chapters.title: Latta and Latta Dosha (Disgrace)
chapters.content_preview: Latta and Latta Dosha (Disgrace)  Latta (Sanskrit: लत्ता), which literally means "kick", is a critical concept in Vedic astrology used primarily to analyze transits (Gocharas) and elect auspicious timings (Muhurtas). It refers to the special malefic mathematical aspect where a tr

chapters.title: The Mathematical Rules of Latta
chapters.content_preview: The Mathematical Rules of Latta  Planets are divided into two categories based on the direction of their "kick": [1, 2]  Puro-Latta (Forward Kick): Malefic planets kick Nakshatras located ahead of them in regular zodiacal sequence.  Prashchat-Latta (Backward Kick): Benefic planet

chapters.title: Scope of Application & Classical Commentary
chapters.content_preview: Scope of Application & Classical Commentary  Phaladeepika by Mantreswara (13th Century C.E.)  Transit Analysis (Gochara): Chapter 26 deals extensively with Nakshatra transits. Mantreswara highlights Latta as a primary hidden affliction that neutralizes otherwise positive planetar

chapters.title: Phaladeepika by Mantreswara (13th Century C.E.)
chapters.content_preview: Phaladeepika by Mantreswara (13th Century C.E.)  Transit Analysis (Gochara): Chapter 26 deals extensively with Nakshatra transits. Mantreswara highlights Latta as a primary hidden affliction that neutralizes otherwise positive planetary transits. If multiple planets kick the same

chapters.title: Muhurta Chintamani by Ramacharya (16th Century C.E.)
chapters.content_preview: Muhurta Chintamani by Ramacharya (16th Century C.E.)  Electional Astrology (Muhurta): Dedicated chapters on marriage and travel note that a Muhurta Nakshatra must be completely free from Latta Dosha. A marriage or venture initiated on a "kicked" star will experience early decay.

chapters.title: Kalamrita by Kalidasa
chapters.content_preview: Kalamrita by Kalidasa  General Transit Neutralization: This text describes exceptions to the rule. For example, it explains that if a planet causing Latta is retrograde, or if the ruling lord of the birth star is exceptionally strong, the severity of the "kick" is significantly d

chapters.title: Ghaat (घात)
chapters.content_preview: Ghaat (घात)  The term Ghaat (घात) means "injury," "harm," or "trauma." This matrix outlines the specific, highly inauspicious astronomical elements that act as energetic blind spots for an individual based entirely on their Janma Rashi (Birth Moon Sign).  📊 The Ghaat Chakra Maste
